summary: Added two README badges showing Code and Data decompilation progress via progress.decomp.club shield endpoints. This was a small documentation/status visibility update with no source or matching changes.
what_changed: Inserted a Code Progress badge near the top of .github/README.md.
Inserted a Data Progress badge near the top of .github/README.md.
Both badges use img.shields.io endpoint badges backed by progress.decomp.club URLs for melee/GALE01/dol with measure=code and measure=data.
Existing build, package publishing, pages publishing, and Discord badges were left unchanged.
smart_moves: Placed progress indicators next to existing repository status badges, making decompilation status visible immediately on the README.
Used shields.io endpoint badges, allowing the displayed values to update from progress.decomp.club without needing repeated README edits.
Separated code and data progress into distinct badges, which matches common decomp tracking needs where function/code matching and data matching progress differ.
decomp_lessons: For decompilation repositories, README status should include both build health and matching/progress health so contributors can quickly assess current project state.
Tracking code and data progress separately is useful because progress in one area does not necessarily imply progress in the other.
External badge endpoints can expose project progress without adding build-system complexity or repository-local generated files.
naming_conventions: Badge labels use concise title-case names: "Code Progress" and "Data Progress" in Markdown alt text, with shields labels "Code" and "Data".
The progress endpoint path identifies the project and target as melee/GALE01/dol, preserving the game/version and binary scope in the status source.
follow_up_queries: Find README or documentation PRs that added or changed project status badges.
Find PRs referencing progress.decomp.club endpoints or GALE01 progress tracking.
Find infrastructure changes related to generated decompilation progress metrics.
